comparative_note: Long sustained magazine feature (Baltic wreck conservation) with named researchers, concrete particulars (felled winter 1627, 90 m, polyethylene glycol, sulphur-to-acid bloom) and a genuine unresolved tension — the writer explicitly complicating Oldenburg's absolute rule. Matches the intellectual temperature and encyclopedic density of the Pantheon exemplar (host-2021-verb2-ELF-040), and the Q-mix (main idea / detail / inference / attitude) with paraphrase-register options is exactly HP long-ELF. Indistinguishable in kind.

ELF gap-completion text: an economics column (resale trade) reading like a real op-ed excerpt — concrete anchors (Rundgang 2021 audit, 1.4 million garments, economist Petra Almen) and real countercurrent (the boom is overstated), close in register to the report-excerpt tone of Education and Pay (host-ver2-2019-verb1-ELF-031). Cloze gaps test vocabulary-in-context, a legitimate ELF task type; prose sits inside the exemplar range.

Short single-paragraph research brief (light-timing vs sleep length) with a named researcher, a hedging quote, and real complication — comparable in expository density to the short authentic fragments Aging Research / Education and Pay (var-2022-1-verb1-ELF-031, host-ver2-2019-verb1-ELF-031). Slightly more rounded than a raw mid-article excerpt but still plausibly a real health-desk short; prompt and paraphrase options are HP-standard.

Short ELF text (movable chairs in a public square) with concrete residue (Faltstrom Square, two summers, urban designer Tobias Wren), a self-limiting quote and genuine claim-tempering. Register and length match the short authentic exemplars (host-ver2-2019-verb1-ELF-031); the reflective closer is essayistic but within HP voice range, not manufactured-tidy. Passes.

Long popular-science/kulturtidskrift piece (tatvuxen furu och allmogebatar) with dense particulars (Nordingra oksstock, 1910s, ~80 rings per inch, felled sixty-three hulls) and a real methodological tension — the named critic Torsten Byle raising survivorship bias, which the author concedes. Argumentative depth and authorial voice match the debate-essay register of the LÄS exemplars (host-2020-verb2-LÄS-020, var-2019-verb1-LÄS-018). Clearly in-register.

Popular-science report (hedgehogs across allotment fences) with solid concrete anchoring (twelve allotment areas, ~9000 camera passes, 2022-2023, three cities) and genuine complication (an opening helps only if neighbouring plots also open; corridors near traffic can be a hazard). Somewhat thinner in detail than las-b4-001 but comfortably inside the exemplar range; tidy rhythm never tips into test-manufactured. Pass.

First-person argumentative column (dialect vs streamed media) pushing back on a recurring media panic, using one three-year study as evidence and closing with an authorial thesis — structurally and tonally very close to the op-ed register of the Sprakbyte exemplar (var-2015-verb2-LÄS-016), which likewise argues a contested claim in the first person. Real tension, educated-adult temperature. Indistinguishable in kind.
